blocks.text: Those who trust in the Lord are like Mount Zion, which cannot be moved, but abides forever.

Ps 125: Prayer for Zion’s purification from evildoers. A Zion song that, unlike most other such songs, prays that sinners be removed from its midst (vv. 4–5). Examples of such purifying judgments are Isa 1.21–28; 65; Zeph 3.11–13. Like other Songs of Ascents (Pss 122; 123; 124; 126; see Ps 120n.), Ps 125 has an opening statement (vv. 1–3) followed by a prayer (vv. 4–5).
As the mountains surround Jerusalem, so the Lord surrounds his people, from this time on and forevermore.
2 : , the hills surrounding Jerusalem are an apt symbol of the powerful and ever‐present in Zion.
For the scepter of wickedness shall not rest on the land allotted to the righteous, so that the righteous might not stretch out their hands to do wrong.
3 : A wielded by an unjust ruler cannot (figuratively) on the land given to the righteous lest they be led to do wrong.
Do good, O Lord, to those who are good, and to those who are upright in their hearts.
4 : Justice means upholding the righteous as well as punishing the wicked.
But those who turn aside to their own crooked ways the Lord will lead away with evildoers. Peace be upon Israel!
5 : is repeated later in this collection, in Ps 128.6.
